title: Scarcity and Abundance
whatItIs:
Scarcity and abundance describe the basic economic and social condition that resources are limited while human wants are not. In abundance, costs fall and access widens; in scarcity, tradeoffs sharpen and exclusion grows.

The topic is useful because it explains behaviour across markets, households, and governments.
whyItMatters:
It matters because a huge amount of politics is really about who gets access to scarce goods: housing, energy, water, capital, time, and attention.

It also matters because abundance changes culture. When something becomes cheap and widespread, people stop organising around it the way they once did.
keyIdeas: Distribution matters as much as total supply
frontier: The frontier is whether AI, automation, and energy systems can create new forms of abundance without producing new bottlenecks elsewhere. The question is bigger than cost reduction; it is about social redesign.
openQuestions: What becomes scarce when one thing becomes abundant?
What do societies do when scarcity shifts rather than disappears?
